rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Further Respond To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, DECEMBER 10, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 at 9:00 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant ExxonMobil Corp.¿s motion to compel plaintiff¿s further response to special interrogatories, set three; requests for admission, set two; and form interrogatories, set two; and request for sanctions: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE WEBB et al VS. BALTIMORE ENNIS LAND CO., INC. et al

rulings.Case Title: BILLY D ROGERS VS. 3M COMPANY, FKA MINNESOTA MINING AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery And Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Answers And Responses
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, DECEMBER 10, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 at 9:00 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Chevron U.S.A. Inc.¿s motion to reopen discovery and to compel plaintiffs¿ further discovery answers and responses: Defendant¿s motion to reopen discovery and allow a discovery motion to be heard past the discovery cutoff date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 2024.050. Defendant¿s motion to compel plaintiffs to provide further responses to requests for admissions is denied. Plaintiffs¿ responses are sufficient in view of defendant¿s requests for admissions nos. 1 and 3. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
